notes: Catalog for reusable SwiftUI/AppKit macOS Lego components. AIWizard should copy canonical Swift source files into generated macOSApp artifacts before writing app-specific glue.

categories.description: Reusable engine, dictionary, keyboard, and smoke-test helpers for native five-letter word guessing games.
Reusable macOS app shell and scene affordances. Planned after the word-game MVP proves the gate.
Reusable SpriteKit scene, input, HUD, and game-loop helpers. Planned after the shell layer is stable.

components.name: WordGameEngine
components.summary: Observable five-letter word guessing engine with duplicate-letter scoring, row advancement, permissive fallback validation, and reset support.

contracts.input: Call inputLetter(_:), deleteLetter(), and submitGuess() from physical or on-screen controls.
contracts.state: Bind rows, currentInput, state, and statusMessage into SwiftUI views.
contracts.validation: Default dictionary allows known answers plus permissive five-letter ASCII fallback so Enter never looks dead.

components.name: WordGameLexicon
components.summary: Inline curated five-letter answer and valid-guess lists that avoid Bundle.module resource pitfalls.

components.name: WordGameKeyboardHandling
components.summary: SwiftUI ViewModifier for focused physical keyboard input using stable onKeyPress signatures for letters, Return, and Delete.

contracts.return: Uses .onKeyPress(.return, phases: .down) with explicit closure parameter.
contracts.delete: Uses .onKeyPress(.delete, phases: .down) with explicit closure parameter.

components.name: WordGameSmokeTests
components.summary: Runtime smoke-test reporter that exercises a wrong guess, reset, and winning guess so the Harness sees useful stdout.

contracts.coverage: Exercises row advancement, reset, and win path without UI automation.

compositionRecipes.name: Native Word Game
assemblyNotes: Copy all selected component files verbatim under Sources/<TargetName>/MChatAIComponents/.
Keep app-specific UI in ContentView.swift or Views/*.swift; do not edit the copied component files.
Call WordGameSmokeTests.printLaunchReport() from the @main App init() after activation setup.
Use wordGameKeyboardHandling(onLetter:onDelete:onSubmit:) on the root playable surface so physical keyboard input works immediately.
Add visible ENTER and DELETE controls in the on-screen keyboard, even when physical keyboard handling is present.

goldenAssemblies.name: Native Word Game Golden Assembly
goldenAssemblies.summary: Minimal SwiftUI composition showing how the copied word-game components wire into ContentView and App init.
notes: Treat example files as glue patterns, not components. They may be adapted to the user's requested visual style.
The selected components themselves should remain byte-for-byte canonical copies.
